label: 3301 Definitions

Short Title, formerly § 9001, , , renumbered § 9001(a) and amended by , (2), , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–114, title IX, § 9001(a)121 Stat. 1288Pub. L. 113–121, title III, § 3016(a)(1)128 Stat. 1289Nov. 8, 2007June 10, 2014“This title [enacting this chapter] may be cited as the ‘National Levee Safety Act of 2007’.”

Purposes of the Safety Program, as added , , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–114, title IX, § 9001(b)Pub. L. 113–121, title III, § 3016(a)(3)128 Stat. 1289June 10, 2014“The purposes of this title [enacting this chapter] are—“(1)to ensure that human lives and property that are protected by new and existing levees are safe;“(2)to encourage the use of appropriate engineering policies, procedures, and technical practices for levee site investigation, design, construction, operation and maintenance, inspection, assessment, and emergency preparedness;“(3)to develop and support public education and awareness projects to increase public acceptance and support of levee safety programs and provide information;“(4)to build public awareness of the residual risks associated with living in levee protected areas;“(5)to develop technical assistance materials, seminars, and guidelines to improve the security of levees of the United States; and“(6)to encourage the establishment of effective State and tribal levee safety programs.”

L. 113–121, title III, § 3016(a)(3)128 Stat. 1289June 10, 2014“The purposes of this title [enacting this chapter] are—“(1)to ensure that human lives and property that are protected by new and existing levees are safe;“(2)to encourage the use of appropriate engineering policies, procedures, and technical practices for levee site investigation, design, construction, operation and maintenance, inspection, assessment, and emergency preparedness;“(3)to develop and support public education and awareness projects to increase public acceptance and support of levee safety programs and provide information;“(4)to build public awareness of the residual risks associated with living in levee protected areas;“(5)to develop technical assistance materials, seminars, and guidelines to improve the security of levees of the United States; and“(6)to encourage the establishment of effective State and tribal levee safety programs.”

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: 1 Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

children.label: i
children.text: constrains water flows;

children.label: ii
children.text: is subject to frequent water loading; and

children.label: iii
children.text: is an integral part of a flood risk reduction system that protects the leveed area from flood waters associated with hurricanes, precipitation events, seasonal high water, and other weather-related events.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: B Exclusion
children.text: The term “canal structure” does not include a barrier across a watercourse.

children.heading: Committee
children.label: 3 Committee
children.text: The term “committee” means the Committee on Levee Safety established by .section 3302(a) of this title

children.heading: Floodplain management
children.label: 4 Floodplain management
children.text: The term “floodplain management” means the operation of a community program of corrective and preventative measures for reducing flood damage.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 5 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.label: A
children.text: to establish the global information system location of the levee;

children.label: B
children.text: to determine the general condition of the levee; and

children.label: C
children.text: to estimate the number of structures and population at risk and protected by the levee that would be adversely impacted if the levee fails or water levels exceed the height of the levee.

children.label: i
children.text: the primary purpose of which is to provide hurricane, storm, or flood protection relating to seasonal high water, storm surges, precipitation, or other weather events; and

children.label: ii
children.text: that is normally subject to water loading for only a few days or weeks during a calendar year.

children.label: I
children.text: constrain water flows;

children.label: II
children.text: are subject to more frequent water loading; and

children.label: III
children.text: do not constitute a barrier across a watercourse; and

children.label: ii
children.text: roadway and railroad embankments, but only to the extent that the embankments are integral to the performance of a flood damage reduction system.

children.label: i
children.text: a roadway or railroad embankment that is not integral to the performance of a flood damage reduction system;

children.label: ii
children.text: a canal constructed completely within natural ground without any manmade structure (such as an embankment or retaining wall to retain water or a case in which water is retained only by natural ground);

children.label: iii
children.text: a canal regulated by a Federal or State agency in a manner that ensures that applicable Federal safety criteria are met;

children.label: I
children.text: that is not a part of a Federal flood damage reduction system;

children.label: II
children.text: that is not recognized under the National Flood Insurance Program as providing protection from the 1-percent-annual-chance or greater flood;

children.label: III
children.text: that is not greater than 3 feet high;

children.label: IV
children.text: the population in the leveed area of which is less than 50 individuals; and

children.label: V
children.text: the leveed area of which is less than 1,000 acres; or

children.label: v
children.text: any shoreline protection or river bank protection system (such as revetments or barrier islands).

children.label: A
children.text: an embankment section;

children.label: B
children.text: a floodwall section;

children.label: C
children.text: a closure structure;

children.label: D
children.text: a pumping station;

children.label: E
children.text: an interior drainage work; and

children.label: F
children.text: a flood damage reduction channel.

children.label: A
children.text: that collectively provide flood damage reduction to a defined area; and

children.label: B
children.text: the failure of 1 of which may result in the failure of the entire system.

children.heading: National levee database
children.label: 10 National levee database
children.text: The term “national levee database” means the levee database established under .section 3303 of this title

children.heading: Participating program
children.label: 11 Participating program
children.text: The term “participating program” means a levee safety program developed by a State, regional district, or Indian tribe that includes the minimum components necessary for recognition by the Secretary.

children.heading: Regional district
children.label: 12 Regional district
children.text: The term “regional district” means a subdivision of a State government, or a subdivision of multiple State governments, that is authorized to acquire, construct, operate, and maintain projects for the purpose of flood damage reduction.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “rehabilitation” means the repair, replacement, reconstruction, removal of a levee, or reconfiguration of a levee system, including a setback levee, that is carried out to reduce flood risk, increase resiliency to extreme weather events, or meet national levee safety guidelines.

children.heading: Inclusions
children.label: B Inclusions
children.text: The term “rehabilitation” includes improvements to a levee in conjunction with any repair, replacement, reconstruction, or reconfiguration.

children.heading: Risk
children.label: 14 Risk
children.text: The term “risk” means a measure of the probability and severity of undesirable consequences.

children.label: A
children.text: a State;

children.label: B
children.text: the District of Columbia;

children.label: C
children.text: the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: State levee safety agency
children.label: 16 State levee safety agency
children.text: The term “State levee safety agency” means the agency of a State that has regulatory authority over the safety of any non-Federal levee in the State.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 17 United States
children.text: The term “United States”, when used in a geographical sense, means all of the States.
